message:
feat(cli): add per-subcommand completion options and exit-code sanitization

Shell completions (cli/completion):
- Introduce subcommand_option_sets() to collect per-subcommand options via
  the Completable trait's subcommand_options() method
- Extend all five shell generators (Bash, Zsh, Fish, PowerShell, Elvish)
  to emit subcommand-specific option completions that activate only when
  the relevant subcommand word is present
- Fix Zsh completion: use `compdef _cmd cmd` (not `compdef cmd`), add
  `-C` flag to _arguments for proper context propagation, switch argument
  spec to `*:: :->args` for correct multi-word completion
- Remove trailing `_cmd` self-invocation line from Zsh output (was causing
  double-execution on shell startup)
- Add integration test asserting subcommand options appear in generated
  completion output

Exit code sanitization (cli/exit, cli/error, bin/asupersync):
- Add ExitCode::is_valid() and ExitCode::sanitize() to clamp arbitrary
  integers into the 0–125 range that avoids collision with shell-reserved
  codes (126=not-executable, 127=not-found, 128+N=signal)
- Wire sanitize() into CliError::exit_code() builder and the top-level
  main() process::exit call so no code path can accidentally emit a
  misleading shell-reserved exit status
- Add tests for validity range checks and sanitization of negative,
  reserved, and valid codes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
